# solution.py
"""MultiAgent_Project_Manager / Team_Collaboration_Manager.

A single-file, dependency-free implementation of a multi-agent project
management and team collaboration system.

The system coordinates work among multiple users (agents) and provides:

* User management with roles and uniqueness guarantees.
* Project management with cascading deletes.
* Task management with assignment, deadlines and status tracking.
* Messaging with threaded replies and attachments.
* A performance dashboard (completion rate, completion time, ratings).
* Reporting (project progress, team performance, individual performance) with
  CSV and PDF export.

Design notes
------------
* Every entity shares a single, globally-incrementing integer id starting at 1.
* All public mutating and reading operations are guarded by one shared
  re-entrant lock, exposed on the manager and every sub-service so that callers
  may reason about atomicity. This keeps the system correct under heavy
  concurrent load.
* Getters return deep copies of stored entities so that callers can never
  mutate internal state by accident.
* The "current time" is supplied through an injectable clock, which makes the
  behaviour fully deterministic and testable.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import csv
import enum
import io
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

__all__ = [
    "TeamCollaborationManager",
    "TaskStatus",
    "UserRole",
    "ReportFormat",
    "ReportType",
    "TeamCollaborationError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "AuthorizationError",
    "DuplicateError",
    "ConflictError",
    "Attachment",
    "User",
    "Project",
    "Task",
    "Message",
    "Rating",
]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exceptions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by this module."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint is violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with current system state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserRole(enum.Enum):
    """Access/responsibility level for a user."""

    ADMIN = "admin"
    PROJECT_MANAGER = "project_manager"
    TEAM_LEAD = "team_lead"
    MEMBER = "member"


class TaskStatus(enum.Enum):
    """Lifecycle status of a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class ReportFormat(enum.Enum):
    """Supported export formats for reports."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(enum.Enum):
    """Kinds of reports the system can produce."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Data entities
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """A file attached to a message."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime


@dataclass
class User:
    """A system user / collaborating agent."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A project that groups tasks and conversations."""

    id: int
    name: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int
    description: str = ""
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work that can be assigned and tracked."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    description: str = ""
    assignee_id: Optional[int] = None
    deadline: Optional[datetime] = None
    status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A message posted against a project or a task."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int] = None
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    parent_id: Optional[int] = None
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """A peer rating given to a user."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str = ""
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
_UNSET = object()
_clone = copy.deepcopy


def _is_blank(value: Any) -> bool:
    """Return True when *value* is not a non-empty/non-whitespace string."""

    return not isinstance(value, str) or value.strip() == ""


def _valid_email(email: Any) -> bool:
    """A deliberately small e-mail check: non-empty and contains an '@'."""

    return isinstance(email, str) and email.strip() != "" and "@" in email


def _resolve_clock(now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]]) -> Callable[[], datetime]:
    """Normalise the injected clock into a zero-argument callable."""

    if now is None:
        return datetime.now
    if callable(now):
        return now
    # A fixed datetime was supplied -- wrap it so callers always call it.
    return lambda: now


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shared context
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Context:
    """Central state shared by every service.

    Holding all state (and the lock and id sequence) in one object lets the
    individual services collaborate -- e.g. deleting a project can cascade into
    tasks and messages -- while still presenting a clean, separated API.
    """

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]]) -> None:
        self.lock = threading.RLock()
        self._now_fn = _resolve_clock(now)
        self._id_counter = 0

        # Primary stores keyed by id.
        self.users: Dict[int, User] = {}
        self.projects: Dict[int, Project] = {}
        self.tasks: Dict[int, Task] = {}
        self.messages: Dict[int, Message] = {}
        self.ratings: Dict[int, Rating] = {}

        # Secondary indexes for fast lookups.
        self.username_index: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self.email_index: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self.assignee_index: Dict[int, set] = {}
        self.project_tasks: Dict[int, set] = {}
        self.project_messages: Dict[int, set] = {}
        self.task_messages: Dict[int, set] = {}
        self.ratee_ratings: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}

    # -- time ----------------------------------------------------------------
    def now(self) -> datetime:
        return self._now_fn()

    # -- ids -----------------------------------------------------------------
    def next_id(self) -> int:
        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter

    # -- internal lookups (return canonical objects, never copies) ----------
    def require_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        user = self.users.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def require_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        project = self.projects.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def require_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        task = self.tasks.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def require_message(self, message_id: int) -> Message:
        message = self.messages.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message


class _Service:
    """Base class giving every service the shared lock and context."""

    def __init__(self, ctx: _Context) -> None:
        self._ctx = ctx

    @property
    def _lock(self) -> threading.RLock:
        return self._ctx.lock


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# User service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _UserService(_Service):
    def register_user(
        self, username: str, email: str, role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER
    ) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            if _is_blank(username):
                raise ValidationError("Username must be a non-empty string")
            if not _valid_email(email):
                raise ValidationError("A valid e-mail address is required")
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
            if username in self._ctx.username_index:
                raise DuplicateError("Username already in use")
            if email in self._ctx.email_index:
                raise DuplicateError("E-mail already in use")

            user_id = self._ctx.next_id()
            user = User(
                id=user_id,
                username=username,
                email=email,
                role=role,
                created_at=self._ctx.now(),
            )
            self._ctx.users[user_id] = user
            self._ctx.username_index[username] = user_id
            self._ctx.email_index[email] = user_id
            return _clone(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._ctx.require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        with self._lock:
            return [_clone(self._ctx.users[i]) for i in sorted(self._ctx.users)]

    def update_user(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        username: Any = _UNSET,
        email: Any = _UNSET,
        role: Any = _UNSET,
    ) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._ctx.require_user(user_id)
            new_username = user.username if username is _UNSET else username
            new_email = user.email if email is _UNSET else email
            new_role = user.role if role is _UNSET else role

            if _is_blank(new_username):
                raise ValidationError("Username must be a non-empty string")
            if not _valid_email(new_email):
                raise ValidationError("A valid e-mail address is required")
            if not isinstance(new_role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            owner = self._ctx.username_index.get(new_username)
            if owner is not None and owner != user_id:
                raise DuplicateError("Username already in use")
            owner = self._ctx.email_index.get(new_email)
            if owner is not None and owner != user_id:
                raise DuplicateError("E-mail already in use")

            if new_username != user.username:
                self._ctx.username_index.pop(user.username, None)
                self._ctx.username_index[new_username] = user_id
            if new_email != user.email:
                self._ctx.email_index.pop(user.email, None)
                self._ctx.email_index[new_email] = user_id

            user.username = new_username
            user.email = new_email
            user.role = new_role
            return _clone(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._ctx.require_user(user_id)

            for project in self._ctx.projects.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError("User owns one or more projects")

            for task_id in self._ctx.assignee_index.get(user_id, set()):
                if self._ctx.tasks[task_id].status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError("User has open (incomplete) tasks")

            self._ctx.username_index.pop(user.username, None)
            self._ctx.email_index.pop(user.email, None)
            self._ctx.assignee_index.pop(user_id, None)
            del self._ctx.users[user_id]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Project service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _ProjectService(_Service):
    def create_project(
        self,
        name: str,
        start_date: datetime,
        end_date: datetime,
        owner_id: int,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            if _is_blank(name):
                raise ValidationError("Project name must be a non-empty string")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not be before start_date")
            self._ctx.require_user(owner_id)

            project_id = self._ctx.next_id()
            project = Project(
                id=project_id,
                name=name,
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
                description=description,
                created_at=self._ctx.now(),
            )
            self._ctx.projects[project_id] = project
            self._ctx.project_tasks[project_id] = set()
            self._ctx.project_messages[project_id] = set()
            return _clone(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._ctx.require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            return [
                _clone(self._ctx.projects[i]) for i in sorted(self._ctx.projects)
            ]

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        name: Any = _UNSET,
        description: Any = _UNSET,
        start_date: Any = _UNSET,
        end_date: Any = _UNSET,
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._ctx.require_project(project_id)
            new_name = project.name if name is _UNSET else name
            new_desc = project.description if description is _UNSET else description
            new_start = project.start_date if start_date is _UNSET else start_date
            new_end = project.end_date if end_date is _UNSET else end_date

            if _is_blank(new_name):
                raise ValidationError("Project name must be a non-empty string")
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not be before start_date")

            project.name = new_name
            project.description = new_desc
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            return _clone(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._ctx.require_project(project_id)

            for task_id in list(self._ctx.project_tasks.get(project_id, set())):
                self._delete_task_internal(task_id)
            for message_id in list(
                self._ctx.project_messages.get(project_id, set())
            ):
                self._delete_message_internal(message_id)

            self._ctx.project_tasks.pop(project_id, None)
            self._ctx.project_messages.pop(project_id, None)
            del self._ctx.projects[project_id]

    # -- cascade helpers -----------------------------------------------------
    def _delete_task_internal(self, task_id: int) -> None:
        task = self._ctx.tasks.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            return
        if task.assignee_id is not None:
            self._ctx.assignee_index.get(task.assignee_id, set()).discard(task_id)
        for message_id in list(self._ctx.task_messages.get(task_id, set())):
            self._delete_message_internal(message_id)
        self._ctx.task_messages.pop(task_id, None)
        self._ctx.project_tasks.get(task.project_id, set()).discard(task_id)
        del self._ctx.tasks[task_id]

    def _delete_message_internal(self, message_id: int) -> None:
        message = self._ctx.messages.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            return
        if message.project_id is not None:
            self._ctx.project_messages.get(message.project_id, set()).discard(
                message_id
            )
        if message.task_id is not None:
            self._ctx.task_messages.get(message.task_id, set()).discard(message_id)
        del self._ctx.messages[message_id]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Task service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _TaskService(_Service):
    def create_task(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        title: str,
        assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
        deadline: Optional[datetime] = None,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._ctx.require_project(project_id)
            if _is_blank(title):
                raise ValidationError("Task title must be a non-empty string")
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline must not precede project start")
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._ctx.require_user(assignee_id)

            task_id = self._ctx.next_id()
            task = Task(
                id=task_id,
                project_id=project_id,
                title=title,
                description=description,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                created_at=self._ctx.now(),
            )
            self._ctx.tasks[task_id] = task
            self._ctx.project_tasks.setdefault(project_id, set()).add(task_id)
            self._ctx.task_messages.setdefault(task_id, set())
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._ctx.assignee_index.setdefault(assignee_id, set()).add(task_id)
            return _clone(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._ctx.require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._ctx.require_project(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self._ctx.project_tasks.get(project_id, set()))
            return [_clone(self._ctx.tasks[i]) for i in ids]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._ctx.require_user(user_id)
            ids = sorted(self._ctx.assignee_index.get(user_id, set()))
            return [_clone(self._ctx.tasks[i]) for i in ids]

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._ctx.require_task(task_id)
            self._ctx.require_user(assignee_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, assignee_id)
            return _clone(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, new_assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._ctx.require_task(task_id)
            self._ctx.require_user(new_assignee_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, new_assignee_id)
            return _clone(task)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._ctx.require_task(task_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, None)
            return _clone(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id: int, deadline: Optional[datetime]) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._ctx.require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._ctx.require_project(task.project_id)
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline must not precede project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return _clone(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._ctx.require_task(task_id)
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")

            now = self._ctx.now()
            if status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            else:  # NOT_STARTED
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            task.status = status
            return _clone(task)

    # -- helpers -------------------------------------------------------------
    def _set_assignee(self, task: Task, assignee_id: Optional[int]) -> None:
        if task.assignee_id is not None:
            self._ctx.assignee_index.get(task.assignee_id, set()).discard(task.id)
        task.assignee_id = assignee_id
        if assignee_id is not None:
            self._ctx.assignee_index.setdefault(assignee_id, set()).add(task.id)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Message service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _MessageService(_Service):
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        body: str,
        project_id: Optional[int] = None,
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
        attachments: Optional[List[Tuple[str, bytes]]] = None,
        parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            if (project_id is None) == (task_id is None):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "Exactly one of project_id or task_id is required"
                )
            attachment_specs = list(attachments or [])
            if _is_blank(body) and not attachment_specs:
                raise ValidationError("Message needs a body or an attachment")

            self._ctx.require_user(author_id)
            if project_id is not None:
                self._ctx.require_project(project_id)
            else:
                self._ctx.require_task(task_id)
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._ctx.require_message(parent_id)

            now = self._ctx.now()
            built = [
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data),
                    uploaded_by=author_id,
                    uploaded_at=now,
                )
                for filename, data in attachment_specs
            ]

            message_id = self._ctx.next_id()
            message = Message(
                id=message_id,
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=built,
                created_at=now,
            )
            self._ctx.messages[message_id] = message
            if project_id is not None:
                self._ctx.project_messages.setdefault(project_id, set()).add(
                    message_id
                )
            else:
                self._ctx.task_messages.setdefault(task_id, set()).add(message_id)
            return _clone(message)

    def edit_message(self, message_id: int, editor_id: int, new_body: str) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._ctx.require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the author may edit a message")
            if _is_blank(new_body) and not message.attachments:
                raise ValidationError("Message needs a body or an attachment")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = self._ctx.now()
            return _clone(message)

    def add_attachment(
        self, message_id: int, user_id: int, filename: str, data: bytes
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._ctx.require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != user_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the author may add attachments")
            if _is_blank(filename):
                raise ValidationError("filename must be a non-empty string")
            message.attachments.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data),
                    uploaded_by=user_id,
                    uploaded_at=self._ctx.now(),
                )
            )
            return _clone(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._ctx.require_project(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self._ctx.project_messages.get(project_id, set()))
            return [_clone(self._ctx.messages[i]) for i in ids]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._ctx.require_task(task_id)
            ids = sorted(self._ctx.task_messages.get(task_id, set()))
            return [_clone(self._ctx.messages[i]) for i in ids]

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, user_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._ctx.require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != user_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the author may delete a message")
            if message.project_id is not None:
                self._ctx.project_messages.get(message.project_id, set()).discard(
                    message_id
                )
            if message.task_id is not None:
                self._ctx.task_messages.get(message.task_id, set()).discard(
                    message_id
                )
            del self._ctx.messages[message_id]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Performance service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _PerformanceService(_Service):
    def _user_tasks(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        ids = self._ctx.assignee_index.get(user_id, set())
        return [self._ctx.tasks[i] for i in ids]

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            self._ctx.require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._user_tasks(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            self._ctx.require_user(user_id)
            durations = [
                (t.completed_at - t.started_at).total_seconds()
                for t in self._user_tasks(user_id)
                if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and t.started_at is not None
                and t.completed_at is not None
            ]
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def submit_rating(
        self,
        rater_id: int,
        ratee_id: int,
        score: int,
        feedback: str = "",
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Rating:
        with self._lock:
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("A user cannot rate themselves")
            if not isinstance(score, int) or isinstance(score, bool):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer between 1 and 5")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
            self._ctx.require_user(rater_id)
            self._ctx.require_user(ratee_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._ctx.require_task(task_id)

            rating_id = self._ctx.next_id()
            rating = Rating(
                id=rating_id,
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback,
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._ctx.now(),
            )
            self._ctx.ratings[rating_id] = rating
            self._ctx.ratee_ratings.setdefault(ratee_id, []).append(rating_id)
            return _clone(rating)

    def _ratings_for(self, user_id: int) -> List[Rating]:
        return [
            self._ctx.ratings[i]
            for i in self._ctx.ratee_ratings.get(user_id, [])
        ]

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        with self._lock:
            self._ctx.require_user(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [_clone(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        with self._lock:
            self._ctx.require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._user_tasks(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            completion_rate = completed / total if total else 0.0

            durations = [
                (t.completed_at - t.started_at).total_seconds()
                for t in tasks
                if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and t.started_at is not None
                and t.completed_at is not None
            ]
            average_completion_time = (
                sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
            )

            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )

            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "average_completion_time": average_completion_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Reports service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _ReportService(_Service):
    def __init__(self, ctx: _Context, performance: _PerformanceService) -> None:
        super().__init__(ctx)
        self._performance = performance

    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        with self._lock:
            self._ctx.require_project(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self._ctx.project_tasks.get(project_id, set()))
            tasks = [self._ctx.tasks[i] for i in ids]
            total = len(tasks)

            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1

            completed = status_counts[TaskStatus.COMPLETED.value]
            completion_rate = completed / total if total else 0.0

            now = self._ctx.now()
            overdue = sum(
                1
                for task in tasks
                if task.deadline is not None
                and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and task.deadline < now
            )

            per_assignee: Dict[int, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
            for task in tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is None:
                    continue
                stats = per_assignee.setdefault(
                    task.assignee_id, {"total_tasks": 0, "completed_tasks": 0}
                )
                stats["total_tasks"] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    stats["completed_tasks"] += 1

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(
        self, project_id: Optional[int] = None
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._ctx.require_project(project_id)
                scope_ids = set(self._ctx.project_tasks.get(project_id, set()))
            else:
                scope_ids = set(self._ctx.tasks.keys())

            scope_tasks = [self._ctx.tasks[i] for i in scope_ids]
            user_ids = sorted(
                {t.assignee_id for t in scope_tasks if t.assignee_id is not None}
            )

            rows = []
            for user_id in user_ids:
                user_tasks = [
                    t for t in scope_tasks if t.assignee_id == user_id
                ]
                rows.append(self._user_row(user_id, user_tasks))

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "scope_project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        with self._lock:
            self._ctx.require_user(user_id)
            ids = sorted(self._ctx.assignee_index.get(user_id, set()))
            tasks = [self._ctx.tasks[i] for i in ids]
            row = self._user_row(user_id, tasks)
            row.update(
                {
                    "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                    "tasks": [_clone(t) for t in tasks],
                }
            )
            return row

    def export_report(self, report: Any, report_format: ReportFormat) -> Any:
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(report, dict):
                raise ValidationError("report must be a report dictionary")
            if not isinstance(report_format, ReportFormat):
                raise ValidationError("report_format must be a ReportFormat")
            if report_format == ReportFormat.CSV:
                return _report_to_csv(report)
            return _report_to_pdf(report)

    # -- helpers -------------------------------------------------------------
    def _user_row(self, user_id: int, tasks: List[Task]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        total = len(tasks)
        completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
        completion_rate = completed / total if total else 0.0

        durations = [
            (t.completed_at - t.started_at).total_seconds()
            for t in tasks
            if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            and t.started_at is not None
            and t.completed_at is not None
        ]
        average_completion_time = (
            sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
        )

        ratings = [
            self._ctx.ratings[i] for i in self._ctx.ratee_ratings.get(user_id, [])
        ]
        rating_average = (
            sum(r.score for r in ratings) / len(ratings) if ratings else 0.0
        )

        return {
            "user_id": user_id,
            "total_tasks": total,
            "completed_tasks": completed,
            "completion_rate": completion_rate,
            "average_completion_time": average_completion_time,
            "rating_average": rating_average,
        }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Export helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _report_to_csv(report: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    buffer = io.StringIO()
    writer = csv.writer(buffer)

    rows = report.get("rows")
    if isinstance(rows, list):
        headers: List[str] = []
        for row in rows:
            if isinstance(row, dict):
                for key in row:
                    if key not in headers:
                        headers.append(key)
        if not headers:
            headers = [key for key in report if key != "rows"]
        writer.writerow(headers)
        for row in rows:
            if isinstance(row, dict):
                writer.writerow([_csv_cell(row.get(h, "")) for h in headers])
        if not rows and not headers:
            writer.writerow(["report_type", report.get("report_type", "")])
        return buffer.getvalue()

    headers = []
    values = []
    for key, value in report.items():
        headers.append(key)
        values.append(_csv_cell(value))
    if not headers:
        headers = ["report_type"]
        values = [report.get("report_type", "")]
    writer.writerow(headers)
    writer.writerow(values)
    return buffer.getvalue()


def _csv_cell(value: Any) -> str:
    if isinstance(value, (dict, list)):
        return str(value)
    return "" if value is None else str(value)


def _report_to_pdf(report: Dict[str, Any]) -> bytes:
    lines = ["Report: {0}".format(report.get("report_type", "report"))]
    for key, value in report.items():
        if key == "report_type":
            continue
        lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, value))
    return _render_pdf(lines)


def _render_pdf(lines: List[str]) -> bytes:
    """Build a minimal, structurally valid single-page PDF document."""

    text_ops = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "72 720 Td", "14 TL"]
    for index, line in enumerate(lines):
        safe = (
            str(line)
            .replace("\\", "\\\\")
            .replace("(", "\\(")
            .replace(")", "\\)")
        )
        if index:
            text_ops.append("T*")
        text_ops.append("({0}) Tj".format(safe))
    text_ops.append("ET")
    content = "\n".join(text_ops)
    content_bytes = content.encode("latin-1", "replace")

    objects = [
        "<</Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R>>",
        "<</Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1>>",
        "<</Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
        "/Contents 4 0 R /Resources <</Font <</F1 5 0 R>>>>>>",
        "<</Length {0}>>\nstream\n{1}\nendstream".format(
            len(content_bytes), content
        ),
        "<</Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica>>",
    ]

    out = "%PDF-1.4\n"
    offsets = []
    for number, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
        offsets.append(len(out.encode("latin-1", "replace")))
        out += "{0} 0 obj\n{1}\nendobj\n".format(number, body)

    xref_offset = len(out.encode("latin-1", "replace"))
    count = len(objects) + 1
    out += "xref\n0 {0}\n".format(count)
    out += "0000000000 65535 f \n"
    for offset in offsets:
        out += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset)
    out += (
        "trailer\n<</Size {0} /Root 1 0 R>>\nstartxref\n{1}\n%%EOF".format(
            count, xref_offset
        )
    )
    return out.encode("latin-1", "replace")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Facade
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """The public entry point that wires every service together.

    All sub-services share a single lock and a single id sequence via the
    shared :class:`_Context`. Public methods delegate to the relevant service
    so the manager stays a thin, well-documented facade.
    """

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None) -> None:
        self._ctx = _Context(now)
        self.users = _UserService(self._ctx)
        self.projects = _ProjectService(self._ctx)
        self.tasks = _TaskService(self._ctx)
        self.messages = _MessageService(self._ctx)
        self.performance = _PerformanceService(self._ctx)
        self.reports = _ReportService(self._ctx, self.performance)

    @property
    def _lock(self) -> threading.RLock:
        return self._ctx.lock

    def now(self) -> datetime:
        return self._ctx.now()

    # -- users ---------------------------------------------------------------
    def register_user(
        self, username: str, email: str, role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER
    ) -> User:
        return self.users.register_user(username, email, role)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        return self.users.get_user(user_id)

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        return self.users.list_users()

    def update_user(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        username: Any = _UNSET,
        email: Any = _UNSET,
        role: Any = _UNSET,
    ) -> User:
        return self.users.update_user(user_id, username, email, role)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        return self.users.delete_user(user_id)

    # -- projects ------------------------------------------------------------
    def create_project(
        self,
        name: str,
        start_date: datetime,
        end_date: datetime,
        owner_id: int,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Project:
        return self.projects.create_project(
            name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description
        )

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        return self.projects.get_project(project_id)

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        return self.projects.list_projects()

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        name: Any = _UNSET,
        description: Any = _UNSET,
        start_date: Any = _UNSET,
        end_date: Any = _UNSET,
    ) -> Project:
        return self.projects.update_project(
            project_id, name, description, start_date, end_date
        )

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        return self.projects.delete_project(project_id)

    # -- tasks ---------------------------------------------------------------
    def create_task(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        title: str,
        assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
        deadline: Optional[datetime] = None,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.create_task(
            project_id, title, assignee_id, deadline, description
        )

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.get_task(task_id)

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        return self.tasks.list_tasks_by_project(project_id)

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        return self.tasks.list_tasks_by_assignee(user_id)

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.assign_task(task_id, assignee_id)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, new_assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.reassign_task(task_id, new_assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.unassign_task(task_id)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id: int, deadline: Optional[datetime]) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.set_deadline(task_id, deadline)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        return self.tasks.update_status(task_id, status)

    # -- messaging -----------------------------------------------------------
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        body: str,
        project_id: Optional[int] = None,
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
        attachments: Optional[List[Tuple[str, bytes]]] = None,
        parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Message:
        return self.messages.post_message(
            author_id, body, project_id, task_id, attachments, parent_id
        )

    def edit_message(self, message_id: int, editor_id: int, new_body: str) -> Message:
        return self.messages.edit_message(message_id, editor_id, new_body)

    def add_attachment(
        self, message_id: int, user_id: int, filename: str, data: bytes
    ) -> Message:
        return self.messages.add_attachment(message_id, user_id, filename, data)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        return self.messages.list_messages_by_project(project_id)

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        return self.messages.list_messages_by_task(task_id)

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, user_id: int) -> None:
        return self.messages.delete_message(message_id, user_id)

    # -- performance ---------------------------------------------------------
    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        return self.performance.get_completion_rate(user_id)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        return self.performance.get_average_completion_time(user_id)

    def submit_rating(
        self,
        rater_id: int,
        ratee_id: int,
        score: int,
        feedback: str = "",
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Rating:
        return self.performance.submit_rating(
            rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback, task_id
        )

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return self.performance.get_ratings_summary(user_id)

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return self.performance.get_user_dashboard(user_id)

    # -- reports -------------------------------------------------------------
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return self.reports.project_progress_report(project_id)

    def team_performance_report(
        self, project_id: Optional[int] = None
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return self.reports.team_performance_report(project_id)

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return self.reports.individual_performance_report(user_id)

    def export_report(self, report: Any, report_format: ReportFormat) -> Any:
        return self.reports.export_report(report, report_format)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Smoke demonstration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _demo() -> None:  # pragma: no cover - illustrative only
    from datetime import timedelta

    manager = TeamCollaborationManager()
    pm = manager.register_user("alice", "alice@example.com", UserRole.PROJECT_MANAGER)
    dev = manager.register_user("bob", "bob@example.com", UserRole.MEMBER)

    start = manager.now()
    project = manager.create_project(
        "Website", start, start + timedelta(days=30), pm.id
    )

    design = manager.create_task(project.id, "Design", assignee_id=dev.id)
    manager.update_status(design.id, TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
    manager.update_status(design.id, TaskStatus.COMPLETED)

    manager.post_message(pm.id, "Great work on the design!", task_id=design.id)
    manager.submit_rating(pm.id, dev.id, 5, "Excellent")

    print("Dashboard:", manager.get_user_dashboard(dev.id))
    report = manager.project_progress_report(project.id)
    print("Progress report:", report)
    print("CSV export:\n", manager.export_report(report, ReportFormat.CSV))


if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    _demo()
